chunks.title: If Else Conditional Statements in Python
chunks.text: Do you remember when it used to rain in our childhood, we used to say that if it rains then we will not go to school.

chunks.title: If Else Conditional Statements in Python
chunks.text: And if it does not rain then we will go to school.

chunks.title: If Else Conditional Statements in Python
chunks.text: In this way, we used to make a decision.

chunks.title: If Else Conditional Statements in Python
chunks.text: We make a lot of decisions in our life.

chunks.title: If Else Conditional Statements in Python
chunks.text: For example, if I am tired then I will not go out to roam.

chunks.title: If Else Conditional Statements in Python
chunks.text: And if I am not tired then I will go out to roam.

chunks.title: If Else Conditional Statements in Python
chunks.text: So this kind of decision making goes on that if you are hungry then order food.

chunks.title: If Else Conditional Statements in Python
chunks.text: If you are not hungry then don't order.

chunks.title: If Else Conditional Statements in Python
chunks.text: If you are hungry and have money in your pocket then order food.

chunks.text: Otherwise, control your hunger.

chunks.title: If Else Conditional Statements in Python
chunks.text: Make something at home and eat.

chunks.title: If Else Conditional Statements in Python
chunks.text: So this kind of decision making goes on in our daily life.

chunks.text: In our Python programming also to do this kind of decision making,

text: Do you remember when it used to rain in our childhood, we used to say that if it rains then we will not go to school. And if it does not rain then we will go to school. In this way, we used to make a decision. We make a lot of decisions in our life. For example, if I am tired then I will not go out to roam. And if I am not tired then I will go out to roam. So this kind of decision making goes on that if you are hungry then order food. If you are not hungry then don't order. If you are hungry and have money in your pocket then order food. Otherwise, control your hunger. Make something at home and eat. So this kind of decision making goes on in our daily life. In our Python programming also to do this kind of decision making, there are if else statements. We can use if, we can use elif, we can use else. How to use all these conditionals in Python programming, I will tell you today. Let's go to the computer screen and let's get started. So guys, I will open my rappel. First of all, it is of day 14. And as you can see here, today's topic is if else statement. Many times in the program, it happens that according to a condition, I will do some work. For example, let's say I take an input from the user. And I say, by the way, I can write int input like this. To take an input as an integer. Because you know that whatever comes from the input, it comes as a string. And if I want to take it in an integer, then I can use the int function. I can do typecasting of these. Suppose I say enter your age. And I am bringing the user's age in A. And I do something like this. Your age is and I will put a comma in some way and print its age. After this, I can display it conditionally if its age is more than 18. That you can drive or you can not drive. One of the two things is fine. Assume that its age is greater than 18. So I will tell him that what you do, I will put a colon like this. I will enter and after this I will say that you can drive. And after this, see I will write the program first. After that, I will explain to you one by one what is what, what space is given, what is indentation. You can not drive. Suppose I wrote 9 in enter your age. Your age is 9. I am running the program again with these two if else statements. If I wrote 9 and hit enter, then see you can not drive. But if I write 90 or 78. The program has not run yet. If I write 78. In fact, I have given a very big number. I wrote 76, your age is 76. You can drive. I will give a little space here. So based on input, we have determined here that a person can drive or not. And now I would like to come here, the tutorial I have written. Sometimes the program needs to check the evaluation of a certain expression. Whether the expression evaluates to true or false.
